timeline.note: Neighbour reports extensive rear extension construction at 14 Porchester Terrace. No planning application visible on portal. Work appears to be a two-storey rear extension with roof terrace.
Site visit scheduled for 14 March.

Inspector visited. Confirmed two-storey rear extension under construction, approximately 6m depth. No planning permission found. Property is within Bayswater Conservation Area. Roof terrace with glass balustrade visible. Significant overlooking of neighbouring gardens. Photos taken.
PCN issued to owner requiring information about the works within 21 days.
Owner's solicitor responds claiming works are permitted development under Class A. However, property is in conservation area — PD rights are restricted.

Legal review confirms: Class A PD rights DO NOT apply in conservation areas for rear extensions of this scale. Two-storey extensions require planning permission. The roof terrace also requires permission. Owner has been advised but construction continues.

Cllr Davidson contacts enforcement team on behalf of 3 residents. Requests urgent update on enforcement action. Notes construction noise at weekends (breach of construction hours condition even if permitted).

case_notes: Unauthorised two-storey rear extension and roof terrace at 14 Porchester Terrace, within Bayswater Conservation Area. Owner claims PD rights but these are restricted in conservation areas. Construction continuing despite being informed permission is required. 4 complaints from neighbouring properties citing overlooking, loss of light, and weekend construction noise. Councillor enquiry received. Legal review confirms enforcement notice appropriate. ENFORCEMENT NOTICE NOT YET ISSUED — 36 days since site visit confirmed breach.

timeline.note: Westminster Conservation Trust reports unauthorised internal alterations at 47 Jermyn Street, Grade II listed building. Original Victorian shopfront being removed and replaced with modern aluminium. Internal ornamental plasterwork and cornicing reportedly being stripped out.

URGENT: Inspector attended same day. Confirmed removal of original Victorian shopfront in progress — approximately 60% removed. Internal works visible: ornamental ceiling rose removed, original cornicing being hacked off. This constitutes a criminal offence under Section 9 of the Planning (Listed Buildings and Conservation Areas) Act 1990. Photos and video taken as evidence.
Temporary Stop Notice served requiring all works to cease immediately. Effective for 28 days.
Police informed of criminal offence — unauthorised works to a listed building. Case reference MET-2026-PL-4421.

Listed Building Enforcement Notice served requiring: (1) cessation of all works, (2) reinstatement of removed shopfront elements, (3) reinstatement of internal plasterwork where possible. Compliance period: 3 months.
Owner's solicitor lodges appeal against the Listed Building Enforcement Notice with the Planning Inspectorate. Claims works are 'like-for-like replacement' not requiring consent.

Heritage officer review: the replacement is NOT like-for-like. Original was timber Victorian shopfront with decorative pilasters; replacement is modern aluminium with no historic character. Appeal unlikely to succeed. However, further damage occurring during appeal period is a concern — original fabric cannot be recovered once destroyed. PROSECUTION FILE BEING PREPARED but not yet submitted to legal. Irreversible heritage loss ongoing.

case_notes: CRIMINAL OFFENCE — unauthorised works to Grade II listed building at 47 Jermyn Street, St James's Conservation Area. Victorian shopfront being replaced with modern aluminium. Internal ornamental plasterwork stripped. Temporary Stop Notice served but owner appealing. Original heritage fabric being irreversibly destroyed. Prosecution file in preparation but NOT YET SUBMITTED to legal — 12 days since criminal offence confirmed. Westminster Conservation Trust and 2 heritage bodies have complained. Press interest likely given Jermyn Street's prominence.

timeline.note: Resident reports 142 Edgware Road (approved as A1 retail) is operating as a late-night shisha lounge. Open until 3am, smoke and noise affecting 12 flats above. Planning permission 19/04221/FULL was for retail use only.

Inspector visited at 10pm. Confirmed: premises operating as shisha lounge/café with indoor smoking area. No planning permission for A3/sui generis use. No late-night licence visible. Significant extraction fan noise. Tobacco smoke odour in common areas of flats above.
PCN issued to premises operator requiring information about the use within 21 days.
No response received within 21 days. PCN deadline missed.
Enforcement Notice served requiring cessation of shisha lounge use and return to A1 retail. Compliance period: 28 days (by 17 April 2026).
Referred to Environmental Health for indoor smoking (Health Act 2006 breach) and noise nuisance investigation.
Site visit at 11pm. Shisha lounge STILL OPERATING despite enforcement notice. Full house, approximately 40 customers. Smoke extraction running. Non-compliance with enforcement notice noted.
Cllr Ahmed contacts team — 7 separate complaints from residents above. Requests prosecution timeline. Ward surgery raised this as top issue.

case_notes: Unauthorised change of use from A1 retail to shisha lounge at 142 Edgware Road. Enforcement Notice served with 28-day compliance (deadline 17 April 2026). Monitoring visit on 10 April confirmed NON-COMPLIANCE — lounge still operating at 11pm with ~40 customers. Indoor smoking breach referred to Environmental Health. 7 complaints from residents, councillor enquiry received. ENFORCEMENT NOTICE DEADLINE IN 2 DAYS (17 April) — if non-compliant, prosecution proceedings should commence. Prosecution file NOT YET PREPARED. Cross-referral to licensing not yet actioned.

timeline.note: Residents association reports new restaurant at 28 Paddington Street breaching multiple conditions of planning permission 23/08104/FULL. Conditions breached: (1) Operating beyond permitted hours (10pm close, observed open until midnight), (2) External seating area not removed by 9:30pm as required, (3) Kitchen extraction system not installed to approved specification — odour and noise complaints.

Inspector visited at 10:30pm. Confirmed: restaurant open and serving, external tables still occupied with diners. Condition 4 requires closure by 10pm. Condition 7 requires external seating removal by 9:30pm. Kitchen extraction unit audibly louder than submitted noise assessment predicted — appears to be a different unit than approved.
Breach of Condition Notice served for conditions 4 (operating hours) and 7 (external seating). Immediate compliance required — there is no right of appeal against a BCN.
Site visit at 10:15pm. Restaurant now closed by 10pm — condition 4 compliance achieved. However, external furniture still out at 10:15pm (should be removed by 9:30pm). Condition 7 still breached.
Independent noise assessment commissioned to verify kitchen extraction compliance with condition 12. Report due by 25 April.

case_notes: Multiple condition breaches at 28 Paddington Street restaurant (planning ref 23/08104/FULL). Breach of Condition Notice served for hours and seating. Partial compliance: operating hours now met, but seating still not removed on time. Kitchen extraction noise/odour remains unresolved — independent assessment pending. 3 complaints from residents association. This is a manageable case progressing through normal enforcement process — no SLA breach but condition 12 (extraction) assessment overdue if not received by 25 April.

timeline.note: Neighbour reports basement excavation at 38 Devonshire Street. Vibration damage to adjacent properties. No planning application found.

Inspector visited. Confirmed major basement excavation in progress. Approximately 2 metres depth, entire footprint. No planning permission. Harley Street Conservation Area. Party wall agreement concerns raised by adjoining owner.
PCN issued. 21-day response required.
Owner's agent responds. Claims works are repairs only. Structural engineer report provided but does not address excavation depth.
Review confirms works exceed any repair claim. Full basement excavation requires planning permission, particularly in conservation area. Enforcement notice recommended.
Enforcement Notice served requiring cessation of excavation and reinstatement. 28-day compliance period (by 17 April).
Site visit. Works appear to have stopped. No machinery on site. However, excavation remains unfilled. Compliance deadline approaching - reinstatement not yet commenced.

case_notes: Unauthorised basement excavation at 38 Devonshire Street in Harley Street Conservation Area. Full basement dig approximately 2 metres deep. Enforcement Notice served 20 March with compliance deadline 17 April. Works have stopped but excavation not yet reinstated. Compliance deadline in 2 days. If not reinstated, prosecution proceedings needed. Adjoining property has reported cracking - structural concerns.

timeline.note: Historic England flags unauthorised replacement of original sash windows with uPVC at 82 Pall Mall, Grade II* listed building.
Urgent inspection. Confirmed 8 original Georgian sash windows on front elevation replaced with uPVC double glazing. Work complete. Grade II* listing - this is a significant heritage harm.
Listed Building Enforcement Notice served requiring removal of uPVC windows and reinstatement of timber sash windows to match originals. 6-month compliance period.
Owner agrees to reinstate. Appointed specialist joinery firm. Timber windows being manufactured.
Progress check. Owner provided evidence of joinery order. Estimated delivery mid-April. Installation scheduled for late April.
Timber windows delivered to site. Installation being arranged. Heritage officer to inspect prior to fitting to verify specification matches originals.

case_notes: Unauthorised uPVC window replacement at 82 Pall Mall, Grade II* listed building. 8 original Georgian sash windows replaced. LBEN served and owner cooperating. Specialist timber replacement windows manufactured and delivered. Heritage officer inspection of new windows scheduled before installation. Compliance expected within enforcement notice timescale. Positive trajectory but requires monitoring to ensure quality of reinstatement matches original detailing.

timeline.note: Resident reports ground floor flat at 115 Great Portland Street being used as short-term holiday let. Constant turnover of guests, noise from wheeled suitcases, parties at weekends. Planning permission 20/07331/FULL is for residential use (C3).

Inspector attended. Property listed on Airbnb as 'Luxury Fitzrovia Flat'. Reviews indicate continuous letting for past 8 months. This exceeds the 90-day limit and constitutes a material change of use.
PCN issued to property owner requiring details of use.
Owner claims only occasional letting. However, Airbnb listing shows 312 nights booked in past year. Screenshots preserved as evidence.
5 complaints total from residents in building. Building management company also complained about wear on common areas.

Evidence strongly supports material change of use from C3 residential to short-term let (sui generis). 312 nights exceeds 90-day threshold. Enforcement notice drafting commenced. Environmental Health referral for noise NOT yet made despite multiple noise complaints from guests.

case_notes: Unauthorised change of use from C3 residential to short-term holiday let at 115 Great Portland Street. Airbnb listing shows 312 nights booked in past year, well exceeding the 90-day temporary use limit. 5 complaints from residents about noise, antisocial behaviour from guests, and common area damage. PCN response was misleading. Enforcement notice being drafted. Cross-referral to Environmental Health for noise complaints NOT yet actioned. Loss of permanent residential accommodation is Westminster's highest enforcement priority.

timeline.note: Residents group reports new cafe on Bell Street not complying with delivery hours condition. Deliveries at 6am, condition requires no deliveries before 7am.
Inspector visited at 6:15am. Confirmed delivery vehicle unloading. Condition 9 of 24/02156/FULL restricts deliveries to 7am-7pm.
BCN served for condition 9 breach. Immediate compliance required.
Officer visited at 6:30am. No deliveries observed. Staff confirmed delivery schedule changed to 7:30am.
Follow-up check. No further complaints. Deliveries now consistently after 7am.
Full compliance confirmed. Complainant notified. Case resolved.

case_notes: Breach of delivery hours condition at cafe on Bell Street. BCN served and compliance achieved. Delivery schedule amended. No further issues. Case resolved satisfactorily.

timeline.note: Neighbour reports satellite dish installed on front elevation of 12 Connaught Square, a Grade II listed building. No listed building consent application found.

case_notes: Satellite dish installation on front elevation of Grade II listed building at 12 Connaught Square, Bayswater Conservation Area. Site visit required to confirm. If confirmed, this requires listed building consent. Relatively minor works but visible from highway in conservation area. Standard priority - site visit to be scheduled within 5 working days.

timeline.note: Westminster Housing Department refers case of residential flat at 67 Warwick Way being used as office space by property management company. Planning permission is C3 residential.
Inspector confirmed. Ground floor flat fitted out as office with 4 desks, server room. No residential use evident. Material change of use to B1 office.
Enforcement Notice served requiring return to residential use. 3-month compliance period.
Office equipment being removed. Owner states residential conversion underway.
Site visit confirms flat restored to residential use. New tenant occupying. Case resolved. Loss of residential accommodation reversed.

case_notes: Successful enforcement against loss of residential accommodation. Flat at 67 Warwick Way converted back from office to residential use following enforcement notice. Westminster's highest enforcement priority - protecting residential stock. Case resolved with positive outcome.

timeline.note: Councillor reports unauthorised removal of ornamental stonework from Grade II listed building at 210 Regent Street. Decorative frieze panels being removed from upper floors. Building is part of the Nash terrace composition.

URGENT inspection same day. Scaffolding erected on upper floors. Confirmed 3 decorative stone frieze panels removed from third floor level. Fragments on scaffold platform. Works ongoing. This constitutes a criminal offence under Section 9.
Temporary Stop Notice served. All works to cease immediately.
Police notified of criminal offence. Case ref MET-2026-PL-4498.

Heritage officer assessment: removed frieze panels are original Regency-period decorative stonework. Irreplaceable. Owner's contractor claims panels were 'unsafe' but no structural survey has been produced. Crown Estate (freeholder) not consulted.

Prosecution file being prepared. 6 days since criminal offence confirmed. 14 working day deadline for submission to legal = by 29 April. Removed panels secured as evidence. Historic England formally objecting. Crown Estate cooperating with enforcement.

case_notes: CRIMINAL OFFENCE — unauthorised removal of Regency-period decorative stonework from Grade II listed building at 210 Regent Street. Part of Nash terrace composition. 3 frieze panels removed, irreplaceable heritage loss. TSN served and police notified. Prosecution file being prepared — on track for 14 working day deadline. Historic England and Crown Estate involved. High-profile location with likely press interest. Councillor Ashworth personally monitoring.

timeline.note: Resident reports new convenience store on Lisson Grove has installed a large illuminated sign not matching the approved drawings. Condition 3 of 25/00891/FULL specified discreet non-illuminated signage. Current sign is a large internally-lit box sign.

case_notes: Alleged breach of signage condition at convenience store on Lisson Grove. Condition 3 required non-illuminated signage but large internally-lit box sign installed. Site visit needed to confirm breach. Standard priority case. If confirmed, BCN likely as signage conditions are straightforward to enforce.

timeline.note: Westminster Planning Watch reports roof terrace construction at 52 Westbourne Park Road. Trellis screens and decking visible from street. Property is in Westbourne Conservation Area.

Inspector visited. Confirmed roof terrace created on flat roof of rear extension. Timber decking, glass balustrade, and 1.8m trellis screens. Overlooks neighbouring gardens. Requires planning permission in conservation area.
PCN issued requiring information about works within 21 days. Response due 15 April.
PCN response deadline approaching (15 April). Owner has been advised by agent that a retrospective application may be invited if terrace can be modified to reduce overlooking.

case_notes: Unauthorised roof terrace at 52 Westbourne Park Road in Westbourne Conservation Area. Decking, glass balustrade, and trellis screens. PCN response due 15 April. Agent has been in contact - retrospective application may be possible if overlooking issues can be addressed through design modifications. If not, enforcement notice to follow.

timeline.note: Residents report ground floor retail unit at 88 Crawford Street converted to a dark kitchen operation. Multiple delivery riders congregating outside. Constant motorcycle noise 11am-midnight. Planning permission 21/03456/FULL is for A1 retail.

Inspector visited. Confirmed premises operating as commercial kitchen producing food for multiple delivery app brands. No retail function. Heavy extraction running. 15-20 delivery riders observed waiting outside.
PCN issued.
Operator responded. Claims use falls within Use Class E (commercial/business/service). However, the specific planning permission restricts to retail (former A1).
Enforcement Notice served. The specific condition restricting use to retail overrides the general Use Class E flexibility. Compliance period: 28 days (by 30 April).

Enforcement notice compliance deadline 30 April. Environmental Health referral made for extraction noise. Licensing check - no food premises registration found, referred to food safety team. 4 complaints from residents. Councillor enquiry received 10 April, response sent within SLA.

case_notes: Unauthorised change from retail to dark kitchen at 88 Crawford Street. Multiple delivery app brands operating from single premises. Heavy rider congregation causing noise and obstruction. Enforcement notice served with 30 April compliance deadline. Cross-referrals made to Environmental Health (extraction noise) and food safety team (no registration). 4 complaints from residents. Use Class E argument rejected as specific retail condition overrides general class.

timeline.note: Report of unauthorised construction at Wigmore Street.
Inspector visited. Breach confirmed. Photographs taken.
PCN issued to owner requiring information within 21 days.

case_notes: unauthorised construction case at Wigmore Street. Listed building — heritage sensitivity. Under investigation.

Inspector visited. Breach confirmed. Photographs taken.

case_notes: breach of conditions case at Seymour Place. Within Fitzrovia Conservation Area. Under investigation.
